text.div:
Chemistry

Test11-JAN-2025Reference RangeUnit
Ketones [Presence] in Urine by Test stripUrine ketone test negative

Urinalysis

Test11-JAN-2025Reference RangeUnit
Glucose [Mass/volume] in Urine by Test strip8.60 - 20mg/dL
Specific gravity of Urine by Test strip1.0 L1.005 - 1.030{nominal}
pH of Urine by Test strip6.55.0 - 8.0pH
Protein [Mass/volume] in Urine by Test strip11.70 - 20mg/dL
Nitrite [Presence] in Urine by Test stripUrine nitrite positive
Hemoglobin [Presence] in Urine by Test stripUrine blood test = +
Leukocyte esterase [Presence] in Urine by Test stripUrine leukocyte test
Leukocytes [#/area] in Urine sediment by Microscopy high power field22.7 H0 - 0/[HPF]
Erythrocytes [#/area] in Urine sediment by Microscopy high power field2.10 - 5/[HPF]
Epithelial cells [#/area] in Urine sediment by Microscopy high power field2.00 - 5/[HPF]
Casts [Presence] in Urine sediment by Light microscopyUrine microscopy: no casts
Mucus [Presence] in Urine sediment by Light microscopyMucus in urine
Bacteria [#/area] in Urine sediment by Microscopy high power fieldFinding of presence of bacteria

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The urine findings indicate a urinary tract infection (UTI) with significant leukocyturia, bacteriuria, positive nitrites, and Klebsiella pneumoniae >100,000 CFU/mL. Presence of blood and protein is likely secondary to infection. Low specific gravity may reflect dilute urine. No ketones or casts detected. Clinical correlation and appropriate antibiotic therapy are recommended.
